This is a substantial, multi-stage design task — defining the kinome, selecting guides, predicting off-targets, and planning construction + sequencing. That warrants a plan you can sign off on before I start fetching data and running alignments. Let me lay one out.

A few defaults I'm building in (flag any you want changed at approval): **human** kinome, **SpCas9 (NGG PAM)**, **4 sgRNAs/gene**, and anchoring guide selection on the **Brunello** genome-wide library (Doench et al. 2016 — already optimized with Azimuth Rule Set 2 on-target and CFD off-target scoring), which is what a real lab does rather than re-deriving guides from scratch. I'll add genuine de-novo off-target analysis on top.

input.human_description: Planning kinome CRISPR KO library design
input.task_summary: Design a genome-wide CRISPR knockout screen targeting the entire human kinome: define the kinase gene set, select optimal SpCas9 sgRNAs, predict off-targets, add controls, and plan library construction + sequencing depth.

feasibility.rationale: Highly feasible. Core approach anchors on the published Brunello genome-wide library (4 optimized sgRNAs/gene, Azimuth Rule Set 2 + CFD) subset to the human protein kinome (~518 kinases, Manning/KinHub). The one step with moderate compute/download risk is genome-wide off-target re-analysis (requires the human genome FASTA + Cas-OFFinder), but this machine (128 CPU) handles it for the ~2,000-2,400 selected guides. Assumptions: human, SpCas9/NGG, 4 guides/gene; adjustable at approval.

steps.description: Install Cas-OFFinder (bioconda) and download the human genome (GRCh38) FASTA. Enumerate genome-wide near-matches for all selected 20-mer+NGG guides allowing up to 3-4 mismatches, then compute CFD off-target and MIT specificity scores per guide, counting 0/1/2/3-mismatch sites and exonic/on-gene hits. Flag guides with high-risk off-targets (high-CFD hits in coding regions). Produce offtarget_report.csv and figure offtarget_specificity.png. (Heaviest step — genome download + alignment.)

steps.title: Guide reselection and controls
steps.description: Replace flagged low-quality/low-specificity guides with better Brunello alternates where available; for genes short of 4 acceptable guides, note de-novo design need. Add control set: ~1,000 non-targeting guides (from Brunello), safe-harbor/intergenic cutting controls, and positive-control essential genes (e.g., RPL/RPS, POLR2A, proteasome) plus pan-essential kinases (CDK1, PLK1) for screen-quality benchmarking. Produce final_kinome_library.csv with a guide-type column.

steps.title: Library construction plan
steps.description: Design the synthesis-ready oligo pool: append 5' and 3' cloning adapters/BsmBI overhangs matching a standard lentiviral backbone (e.g., lentiGuide-Puro / lentiCRISPRv2), verify no internal BsmBI sites, and compute pool complexity. Specify cloning coverage targets (electroporation at >=300x representation), PCR amplification cycles, and Gibson/Golden-Gate ligation parameters. Produce oligo_pool_synthesis.csv and construction_protocol.md.

steps.title: Transduction and sequencing depth planning
steps.description: Compute the screen's scale parameters: cell numbers for maintaining >=500x guide representation at the target MOI (~0.3, accounting for puromycin survival), number of conditions x replicates x timepoints, and NGS read budget at >=300-1,000 reads/guide. Build a depth calculator covering readout PCR, sample multiplexing on a NovaSeq lane, and total reads/Gb required. Produce sequencing_depth_plan.csv and figure coverage_vs_cells.png.

steps.title: Compile design report
steps.description: Assemble a self-contained design report (design_report.html or .md) documenting: kinome definition and scope, guide-selection rationale and QC thresholds, off-target methodology and flagged guides, final library composition (targeting + controls), construction protocol, and sequencing depth math — with all figures embedded and every parameter stated. Include a README listing all deliverable files. Large raw tables referenced by name, not linked.

content.content:
<skill-metadata name="crispr-screen-analysis" source="anthropic" note="read-only — fork under a new name to modify" />

# CRISPR Screen Analysis

Comprehensive toolkit for pooled CRISPR knockout/activation screen analysis, from raw FASTQ processing through gene-level hit calling, pathway enrichment, and multi-method comparison via PPI networks. Covers the complete analysis lifecycle including sgRNA counting, CNV correction, essentiality scoring, editing outcome quantification, and downstream functional interpretation.

## When to Use This Skill

- Counting sgRNA reads from FASTQ files against a library (Brunello, GeCKO, AVANA, KY, custom)
- Identifying essential, depleted, or enriched genes from CRISPR screen count tables
- Correcting copy-number variation bias in screen data
- Analyzing CRISPR amplicon editing outcomes (indels, cut sites, editing efficiency)
- Running pathway enrichment (KEGG, GO BP, Reactome) on screen hits
- Comparing hit-calling methods (MAGeCK RRA/MLE, BAGEL2, DrugZ) via PPI networks
- Detecting barcodes in GPS/ORFeome pooled screens
- Orienting CRISPR amplicon reference sequences
- Mentions of: MAGeCK, BAGEL2, DrugZ, CRISPRcleanR, CRISPResso2, MAGeCKFlute, BioGRID
- Working with: FASTQ, BAM, count tables (.count.txt), sgRNA libraries, BioGRID PPI files

## How to Use This Skill

1. Identify your analysis stage from the approaches below
2. Use the Quick Decision Tree to select the right tool
3. Follow the Quick Start commands for immediate results
4. Consult detailed reference files for variations, parameters, and troubleshooting

## Approach 1: sgRNA Counting (MAGeCK count / Python)

Count sgRNA reads from FASTQ files against an sgRNA library to produce a count matrix.

### Quick Start

```bash
# MAGeCK count (standard approach)
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute mageck count \
    -l library_mageck.txt --fastq ctrl.fastq.gz treat.fastq.gz \
    --sample-label control,treatment -n screen_counts --trim-5 AUTO 2>&1

# Python scaffold-anchored counting (when MAGeCK unavailable)
python scripts/count_sgrnas.py --library library.csv \
    --fastqs ctrl.fastq.gz treat.fastq.gz --samples control treatment \
    --scaffold GTTTAAGAGCTA --prepend-base G --output counts.tsv
```

**CRITICAL**: MAGeCK library must be headerless 3-col TSV (sgRNA_id, sequence, gene). Use `--trim-5 AUTO`, never a sequence string. Always check Gini index and mapping rate in QC output.

**Detailed workflows**: See `mageck_workflow.md` and `python_sgrna_counting_workflow.md`

## Approach 2: CNV Correction (CRISPRcleanR)

Correct copy-number variation bias using unsupervised CBS before downstream analysis.

### Quick Start

```bash
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute Rscript scripts/crisprcleanr_pipeline.R \
    count_table.count.txt  auto 30 1 screen
```

**CRITICAL**: Remap sgRNA sequences to library CODE identifiers before CRISPRcleanR functions. Without this, all sgRNAs are silently dropped (0-row output).

**Detailed workflow**: See `crisprcleanr_workflow.md`

## Approach 3: Gene-Level Hit Calling (MAGeCK test/MLE, BAGEL2, DrugZ)

Identify significant genes from count data using complementary statistical methods.

### Quick Start

```bash
# MAGeCK RRA (two-group comparison)
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute mageck test \
    -k counts.txt -t treatment -c control -n mageck_rra

# MAGeCK MLE (design matrix, beta scores)
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute mageck mle \
    -k counts.txt -d design_matrix.txt -n mle_output --norm-method none

# BAGEL2 Bayes Factor
mamba run -n bioinformatics-core python scripts/bagel2_bayes_factor.py \
    --count-table counts.txt --screen-col treatment --reference-col control \
    --essential essential_genes_v2.txt --nonessential nonessential_genes.txt \
    --output bagel2_results.tsv

# DrugZ sliding-window Z-score
mamba run -n bioinformatics-core python scripts/drugz_reimpl.py \
    -i counts.tsv -o drugz_results.tsv -c control -x treatment
```

**Detailed workflows**: See `mageck_workflow.md`, `bagel2_workflow.md`, `drugz_workflow.md`

## Approach 4: Pathway Enrichment (MAGeCKFlute)

Run KEGG, GO BP, Reactome enrichment on MAGeCK output; compare methods.

### Quick Start

```bash
# Patch depmap for offline use (REQUIRED, run once)
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute Rscript scripts/patch_depmap.R

# FluteRRA analysis
mamba run -n mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute Rscript scripts/run_flute_rra.R \
    gene_summary.txt sgrna_summary.txt /tmp/outputs flute_rra
```

**Detailed workflow**: See `mageckflute_workflow.md`

## Approach 5: Editing Outcome Analysis (CRISPResso2 / pysam)

Analyze CRISPR amplicon editing outcomes, cut sites, and indel profiles.

### Quick Start

```bash
# CRISPResso2 (standard amplicon analysis)
mamba run -n crispresso2 CRISPResso --fastq_r1 reads.fastq \
    --amplicon_seq "$AMPLICON" --guide_seq "$GUIDE" --output_folder /tmp/out --name sample

# pysam deletion-to-cut-site distance
python3 -c "
import pysam
bam = pysam.AlignmentFile('sample.bam', 'rb')
for read in bam.fetch():
    if 'D' in (read.cigarstring or ''):
        # Parse CIGAR for deletion positions
        pass
"
```

**CRITICAL**: `--guide_seq` takes 20bp spacer only (no PAM). Sequences must be uppercase. `sgRNA_cut_points` in JSON output is 0-based.

**Detailed workflows**: See `crispresso2_workflow.md` and `pysam_cutsite_workflow.md`

## Approach 6: Reference Orientation (crisprseq)

Orient CRISPR amplicon reference so protospacer appears on forward strand.

### Quick Start

```bash
python3 scripts/orient_reference.py reference.fasta PROTOSPACER_SEQUENCE
```

**Detailed workflow**: See `crisprseq_workflow.md`

## Approach 7: PPI Network Comparison

Compare hit-calling methods by BioGRID protein-protein interaction connectivity.

### Quick Start

```bash
mamba run -n bioinformatics-core python scripts/crispr_ppi_analysis.py \
    --biogrid biogrid.csv --bagel2 bagel2.bf --drugz drugz.txt \
    --mle mle.gene_summary.txt --rra rra.gene_summary.txt --mode all --outdir /tmp/outputs
```

**Detailed workflow**: See `ppi_network_workflow.md`

## Approach 8: GPS/ORFeome Barcode Detection

Calculate barcode detection percentages from pooled screen FASTQ reads.

### Quick Start

```bash
python3 scripts/count_barcodes.py --orfs orfs.csv --reads-dir reads/ \
    --output gene_barcode_counts.txt --min-barcodes 5
```

**CRITICAL**: Barcodes almost always appear as reverse complements in reads. Forward-only search yields ~0% matches.

**Detailed workflow**: See `barcode_detection_workflow.md`

## Tool Selection Guide

| Factor | MAGeCK RRA | MAGeCK MLE | BAGEL2 | DrugZ |
|--------|-----------|-----------|--------|-------|
| Input | Count table | Count table + design matrix | Count table + ref genes | Count table |
| Output metric | RRA score, LFC, FDR | Beta score, Z, Wald FDR | Bayes Factor | normZ, FDR |
| Multi-factor design | No | Yes | No | No |
| Reference gene sets | Not required | Not required | Required (CEG/NEG) | Not required |
| Deterministic | Yes | Yes | Yes (analytic mode) | Yes |
| Best for | Simple 2-group comparison | Complex designs, beta scores | Essentiality classification | Synthetic lethality |
| Speed (70k sgRNAs) | ~1 min | ~5-30 min | ~2-70s (mode-dependent) | ~10s |

## Quick Decision Tree

**What is your analysis stage?**
- Raw FASTQ files -> **Approach 1** (MAGeCK count / Python counting)
- Count table, need CNV correction -> **Approach 2** (CRISPRcleanR) then Approach 3
- Count table, gene-level hits -> **Approach 3** (MAGeCK/BAGEL2/DrugZ)
  - Simple ctrl vs treat -> MAGeCK test (RRA)
  - Multi-factor design -> MAGeCK MLE
  - Essentiality classification -> BAGEL2
  - Synthetic lethality Z-scores -> DrugZ
- Gene hits, need pathway enrichment -> **Approach 4** (MAGeCKFlute)
- Amplicon sequencing, editing analysis -> **Approach 5** (CRISPResso2/pysam)
- Determine reference orientation -> **Approach 6** (crisprseq orient)
- Compare methods by PPI connectivity -> **Approach 7** (networkx + BioGRID)
- Barcode-based pooled screen -> **Approach 8** (barcode detection)

## Edge Cases

1. **MAGeCKFlute offline failure**: `library(MAGeCKFlute)` fails with `'crispr_22Q1' not found in ExperimentHub`. Run `Rscript scripts/patch_depmap.R` once per environment before any MAGeCKFlute usage.

2. **CRISPRcleanR sequence-to-CODE remapping**: Built-in libraries use CODE identifiers, not sequences. Map via `seq2code <- setNames(lib$CODE, lib$seq)` before calling any ccr.* function.

3. **MAGeCK gene-test-fdr-threshold**: Controls the RRA alpha cutoff (which sgRNAs count as "good" in the `goodsgrna` column), NOT output filtering -- all genes always appear in `gene_summary.txt`. Default 0.25 is appropriate for most analyses.

4. **Pre-normalized input**: Use `--norm-method none` for CRISPRcleanR-normalized data in MAGeCK. Float values need rounding to integers for `mageck test`.

5. **DrugZ not installable**: Not on PyPI or conda. Use bundled `drugz_reimpl.py`. Treatment flag is `-x` (not `-t`).

6. **BAGEL2 BF sign convention**: BF > 0 = essential (depleted). Negate for enrichment analysis. BF and DrugZ normZ are anti-correlated (expected Spearman ~ -0.78).

7. **CRISPResso2 guide_seq**: Provide 20bp spacer only, no PAM. Must be uppercase. Cut points in JSON are 0-based.

8. **GPS/ORFeome barcode orientation**: Barcodes appear as reverse complements in reads. Forward-only grep yields ~0%.

9. **Excel gene name corruption**: SEPT7->"Sep 07", MARCH1->"Mar 01". Use library annotation gene names, not count file gene names.

10. **Conda environments**: Use `mageck_crisprcleanr_mageckflute` for R/MAGeCK, `bioinformatics-core` for Python, `crispresso2` for CRISPResso2. Never use `mamba activate` directly -- use `mamba run -n <env>`.

## Best Practices

1. Always inspect input data before analysis: `head -5 file.tsv`, check column names, verify delimiters
2. Verify fold-change direction: known essential genes (ACTB, PCNA, PLK1) should show negative LFC
3. Run multiple methods (MAGeCK + BAGEL2 + DrugZ) and compare hit overlap for robust calls
4. Use `EnrichAnalyzer(pvalueCutoff=1)` to retain all enrichment terms for comparative analysis
5. Set `display=FALSE` in all CRISPRcleanR/MAGeCKFlute functions for batch/headless mode
6. For GSEA, add tie-breaking noise: `geneList + rnorm(n, 0, 1e-10)` to prevent fgsea crashes
7. Always exclude non-targeting controls (gene=none/safe/NonTargeting) before counting DrugZ hits
8. Use `type="KEGG+REACTOME+GOBP+Complex"` for MAGeCKFlute enrichment in offline environments
9. Check screen quality via Cohen's D between essential/non-essential LFC distributions (D > 1.0 = good)
10. For large MLE runs (>50k sgRNAs), use `--permutation-round 0 --threads 4` to avoid timeout

## Workflow Reference Files

| File | Contents | When to Read |
|------|----------|--------------|
| `mageck_workflow.md` | MAGeCK count/test/MLE, library prep, QC metrics | sgRNA counting and gene-level testing |
| `crisprcleanr_workflow.md` | CRISPRcleanR CNV correction, normalization | Copy-number bias correction |
| `bagel2_workflow.md` | BAGEL2 BF computation, enrichment, network analysis | Bayesian essentiality analysis |
| `drugz_workflow.md` | DrugZ Z-score, FASTQ-to-results pipeline | Synthetic lethality scoring |
| `mageckflute_workflow.md` | MAGeCKFlute enrichment, FluteRRA/MLE, Z-scores | Pathway enrichment and method comparison |
| `crispresso2_workflow.md` | CRISPResso2 editing analysis, cut sites, UMI workflow | Amplicon editing outcomes |
| `pysam_cutsite_workflow.md` | pysam CIGAR parsing, guide inference, cut-site calc | Deletion-to-cut-site distance |
| `crisprseq_workflow.md` | nf-core/crisprseq reference orientation | Amplicon reference orientation |
| `ppi_network_workflow.md` | BioGRID PPI, networkx, method comparison | PPI connectivity comparison |
| `barcode_detection_workflow.md` | GPS/ORFeome barcode detection and per-gene counts | Barcode-based screen analysis |
| `troubleshooting.md` | All troubleshooting across all tools | When encountering errors |

## CLI Scripts

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `bagel2_bayes_factor.py` | Compute BAGEL2 BF (analytic/bootstrap/CV/empirical) |
| `bagel2_drugz.py` | BAGEL2 + DrugZ comparison with Spearman correlation |
| `bagel2_enrichment_analysis.R` | Network edges, connectivity, significance via MAGeCKFlute |
| `bagel2_network_permutation.py` | Permutation-based network significance |
| `bagel2_network_zscore.py` | Z-test pathway co-membership network |
| `crisprcleanr_pipeline.R` | Complete CRISPRcleanR CNV correction pipeline |
| `drugz_reimpl.py` | Standalone DrugZ reimplementation (offline) |
| `count_sgrnas.py` | Scaffold-anchored sgRNA counting from FASTQs |
| `analyze_counts.py` | Fold change, Gini, detection stats from count matrices |
| `crispr_ppi_analysis.py` | Multi-method PPI network comparison |
| `count_barcodes.py` | Per-gene barcode detection from pooled screen reads |
| `detect_barcodes.py` | Barcode detection percentage (grep-based) |
| `orient_reference.py` | CRISPR amplicon reference orientation |
| `fasta_to_fastq.py` | FASTA to FASTQ conversion with dummy quality |
| `patch_depmap.R` | Fix MAGeCKFlute offline depmap loading |
| `mageckflute_rra_selection.R` | FluteRRA selection ranking |
| `run_flute_rra.R` | FluteRRA KEGG pathway wrapper |
| `depleted_gene_gobp_enrichment.R` | GO BP enrichment on depleted genes |
| `compare_crispr_methods.R` | Multi-method enrichment comparison |
| `network_hit_selection.py` | Network-based NB scoring across methods |
| `extract_pathways.R` | Export pathway data from MAGeCKFlute |
| `extract_reference_genes.R` | Extract CEG/NEG from CRISPRcleanR |
| `match_library.R` | Identify matching CRISPRcleanR library |
| `export_library.R` | Export library as MAGeCK-compatible file |
| `compute_gini.py` | Replicate MAGeCK Gini index calculation |
| `compute_cv.py` | Coefficient of variation for sgRNA counts |
| `build_library_from_crisprcleanr.R` | Extract library from CRISPRcleanR |
| `convert_brunello_to_mageck.py` | Brunello library format conversion |
| `add_baseline_column.py` | Add synthetic baseline for FluteMLE offline |

## Key Resources

- [MAGeCK Documentation](https://sourceforge.net/p/mageck/wiki/Home/)
- [BAGEL2 GitHub (hart-lab)](https://github.com/hart-lab/bagel)
- [DrugZ GitHub (hart-lab)](https://github.com/hart-lab/drugz)
- [CRISPRcleanR GitHub](https://github.com/francescojm/CRISPRcleanR)
- [CRISPResso2 GitHub](https://github.com/pinellolab/CRISPResso2)
- [MAGeCKFlute Bioconductor](https://bioconductor.org/packages/MAGeCKFlute/)
- [nf-core/crisprseq](https://nf-co.re/crisprseq)
- [BioGRID Database](https://thebiogrid.org/)
- [pysam Documentation](https://pysam.readthedocs.io/)

## Next Steps After CRISPR Screen Analysis

- **Validation experiments**: Prioritize top hits for individual validation (growth curves, competition assays)
- **Network analysis**: Map hits to STRING/BioGRID PPI networks for pathway context
- **Integration**: Cross-reference with DepMap/CERES data for known essentiality
- **Visualization**: Generate volcano plots, rank plots, pathway maps via MAGeCKFlute or custom R scripts

input.code:
import requests, io, time
uids = sorted(set(kh['UniProtID'].tolist()) - {''})
print("UniProt IDs to map:", len(uids))

# UniProt ID-mapping via stream/search: query accessions in batches, get gene name + GeneID (Entrez)
def fetch_batch(batch):
    q = " OR ".join(f"accession:{a}" for a in batch)
    url = "https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/search"
    params = {"query": q, "format":"tsv", "size":500,
              "fields":"accession,gene_primary,gene_synonym,xref_geneid,protein_name"}
    r = requests.get(url, params=params, timeout=60); r.raise_for_status()
    return pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(r.text), sep="\t")

frames=[]
for i in range(0,len(uids),80):
    b=uids[i:i+80]
    frames.append(fetch_batch(b)); time.sleep(0.3)
up = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
print(up.shape, up.columns.tolist())
print(up.head(3).to_string())

input.code:
up = up.rename(columns={'Entry':'UniProtID','Gene Names (primary)':'Gene_primary',
                        'Gene Names (synonym)':'Synonyms','GeneID':'Entrez','Protein names':'Protein'})
up['Entrez'] = up['Entrez'].astype(str).str.rstrip(';').str.split(';').str[0].replace('nan','')
up['Gene_primary']=up['Gene_primary'].fillna('')

m = kh.merge(up[['UniProtID','Gene_primary','Synonyms','Entrez']], on='UniProtID', how='left')
# Authoritative symbol: prefer UniProt primary gene name; fall back to KinHub HGNC
m['Symbol'] = m['Gene_primary'].where(m['Gene_primary'].astype(bool), m['HGNC'])
m['Symbol'] = m['Symbol'].where(m['Symbol'].astype(bool), m['Manning_Name'])

# collapse to unique GENE level (dual-domain kinases appear twice) -> keep gene with group/family
kinome = (m.sort_values('Symbol')
            .groupby('Symbol', as_index=False)
            .agg({'Manning_Name':'first','Group':'first','Family':'first','SubFamily':'first',
                  'UniProtID':'first','Entrez':'first','Synonyms':'first','Kinase_Name':'first'}))
print("Unique kinase genes:", len(kinome))
print("Missing Entrez:", (kinome['Entrez']=='').sum())
print("Missing symbol:", (kinome['Symbol']=='').sum())
# show the formerly-blank-HGNC ones now resolved
print(kinome[kinome['Manning_Name'].isin(['ZAK','MLK4','SgK494','SgK223','GTF2F1'])][['Symbol','Manning_Name','Group','Entrez']].to_string())

    syns = str(r['Synonyms']).replace('nan','').split()

# Update kinome master with de-novo flag + save
denovo = ['GRK1','STK38','PRKY','CDK8']
kinome['found']=~kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)
kinome['needs_denovo_design']=kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)
kinome.to_csv('kinome_genes.csv', index=False)

# Figure: guides-per-gene summary + coverage by group
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(13,5))
# Left: coverage bar per group (found vs de-novo needed)
grp_tot = kinome.groupby('Group').size()
grp_found = kinome.groupby('Group')['found'].sum()
grp_miss = grp_tot - grp_found
order = grp_tot.sort_values(ascending=False).index
ax=axes[0]
ax.bar(order, grp_found[order], color='#2c7fb8', edgecolor='black', lw=0.5, label='In Brunello (4 guides)')
ax.bar(order, grp_miss[order], bottom=grp_found[order], color='#d95f0e', edgecolor='black', lw=0.5, label='Needs de-novo design')
ax.set_ylabel('Kinase genes'); ax.set_title('Brunello coverage of kinome by group')
ax.legend(fontsize=9); ax.set_xticklabels(order, rotation=30, ha='right')
for s in ['top','right']: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)

# Right: guides per gene histogram (all 4)
ax=axes[1]
gpg_final = kin_guides.groupby('Kinome_Symbol').size()
vc = gpg_final.value_counts().sort_index()
bars=ax.bar(vc.index.astype(str), vc.values, color='#2ca25f', edgecolor='black', width=0.6)
ax.bar_label(bars, padding=2)
ax.set_xlabel('sgRNAs per gene'); ax.set_ylabel('Number of genes')
ax.set_title(f'sgRNA coverage: {len(kin_guides)} guides across {gpg_final.size} genes')
for s in ['top','right']: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)
fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig('guides_per_gene.png', dpi=150)
print("Coverage: %d/%d genes in Brunello (4 guides each); %d need de-novo: %s"%(
    kinome['found'].sum(), len(kinome), len(denovo), denovo))
print("Total kinase sgRNAs selected:", len(kin_guides))

content.thinking: Now Step 3 — on-target QC. Compute GC, poly-T, homopolymer runs, BsmBI sites, CDS percentile, Rule Set 2 distribution.

content.text: **Step 3 — on-target QC.** Computing GC content, poly-T terminators, homopolymer runs, BsmBI/Esp3I cloning conflicts, and profiling the Rule Set 2 scores for all 2,072 guides.

input.code:
import re
g = kin_guides.copy()
seqs = g['sgRNA_sequence'].str.upper()

g['GC_frac'] = seqs.apply(lambda s: (s.count('G')+s.count('C'))/len(s))
g['GC_pct'] = (g['GC_frac']*100).round(1)
# poly-T terminator: TTTT anywhere (RNA Pol III termination signal) - check spacer
g['polyT_TTTT'] = seqs.str.contains('TTTT')
# longest homopolymer run
def maxhomo(s):
    return max(len(m.group(0)) for m in re.finditer(r'(.)\1*', s))
g['max_homopolymer'] = seqs.apply(maxhomo)
# BsmBI / Esp3I site CGTCTC (and revcomp GAGACG) - conflicts with Golden Gate cloning
def has_site(s, site, rc):
    return (site in s) or (rc in s)
g['BsmBI_site'] = seqs.apply(lambda s: has_site(s,'CGTCTC','GAGACG'))
# Also check BsmBI in the full context (spacer only matters for cloning insert, but flag spacer)
# Rule Set 2 score bins
g['RS2_score'] = g['RuleSet2_score']

print("=== On-target QC summary (n=%d guides) ==="%len(g))
print("GC%%: mean=%.1f  median=%.1f  min=%.1f  max=%.1f"%(g['GC_pct'].mean(),g['GC_pct'].median(),g['GC_pct'].min(),g['GC_pct'].max()))
print("GC outside 20-80%%:", ((g['GC_pct']<20)|(g['GC_pct']>80)).sum())
print("polyT (TTTT) terminators:", g['polyT_TTTT'].sum())
print("homopolymer run >=5:", (g['max_homopolymer']>=5).sum(), " >=4:", (g['max_homopolymer']>=4).sum())
print("BsmBI/Esp3I sites in spacer:", g['BsmBI_site'].sum())
print("RS2 score: mean=%.3f median=%.3f min=%.3f max=%.3f"%(g['RS2_score'].mean(),g['RS2_score'].median(),g['RS2_score'].min(),g['RS2_score'].max()))
print("RS2 < 0.2 (low):", (g['RS2_score']<0.2).sum(), " | RS2 < 0.4:", (g['RS2_score']<0.4).sum())

input.code:
# Composite on-target quality flag
g['flag_homopolymer'] = g['max_homopolymer']>=5
g['flag_lowRS2'] = g['RS2_score']<0.4   # (none, but keep column)
g['flag_GC_extreme'] = (g['GC_pct']<25)|(g['GC_pct']>75)
g['ontarget_flag'] = g[['polyT_TTTT','BsmBI_site','flag_homopolymer','flag_lowRS2','flag_GC_extreme']].any(axis=1)
print("Guides with >=1 on-target flag:", g['ontarget_flag'].sum())
print(g[['flag_homopolymer','flag_GC_extreme','polyT_TTTT','BsmBI_site','flag_lowRS2']].sum().to_string())
# how many genes have >=1 flagged guide, and do any genes lose all 4?
flagged_per_gene = g.groupby('Kinome_Symbol')['ontarget_flag'].sum()
print("\nGenes with >=1 flagged guide:", (flagged_per_gene>0).sum())
print("Genes with all 4 flagged:", (flagged_per_gene==4).sum())
print("Max flagged guides in one gene:", flagged_per_gene.max())

qc_cols = ['sgRNA_id','Kinome_Symbol','Group','sgRNA_sequence','PAM','RS2_score','GC_pct',
           'max_homopolymer','polyT_TTTT','BsmBI_site','flag_homopolymer','flag_GC_extreme',
           'flag_lowRS2','ontarget_flag','exon']
g[qc_cols].to_csv('guide_ontarget_qc.csv', index=False)
print("\nSaved guide_ontarget_qc.csv")
kin_guides_qc = g  # keep for later

import pickle, re
mm_scores = pickle.load(open('/tmp/cfd_mismatch_score.pkl','rb'))
pam_scores = pickle.load(open('/tmp/cfd_pam_scores.pkl','rb'))

def revcomp(s):
    return s.translate(str.maketrans('ACGTacgt','TGCAtgca'))[::-1]

def cfd_score(wt, off, pam):
    """wt=intended 20nt spacer, off=observed 20nt, pam=2nt (last 2 of NGG site). Doench 2016 CFD."""
    score = 1.0
    wt=wt.upper(); off=off.upper()
    for i,(w,o) in enumerate(zip(wt, off)):
        if w==o: continue
        # key uses RNA guide base (complement pairing notation): r<comp of off? >
        # CRISPOR convention: key = 'r'+WT_as_RNA + ':d' + complement(off_DNA) ... use standard impl:
        key = 'r'+w+':d'+revcomp(o)+','+str(i+1)
        score *= mm_scores.get(key, 1.0)
    score *= pam_scores.get(pam[-2:], 1.0)
    return score

# sanity: perfect match CFD=1.0 with GG pam
test='GACCCGGAGAGCAGACGAGA'
print("perfect CFD:", cfd_score(test,test,'GG'))
# one mismatch
off=list(test); off[0]='T'; off=''.join(off)
print("1mm pos1 CFD:", round(cfd_score(test,off,'GG'),4))
print("pam AG:", round(cfd_score(test,test,'AG'),4))

input.code:
def revcom(s):
    bc={'A':'T','C':'G','G':'C','T':'A','U':'A'}
    return ''.join(bc[b] for b in s[::-1])

def cfd_score(guide20, offtarget20, pam2):
    """Exact Doench2016 CFD. guide20=intended spacer, offtarget20=genomic 20mer, pam2=last 2 of PAM."""
    sg = offtarget20.upper().replace('T','U')
    wt = guide20.upper().replace('T','U')
    score=1.0
    for i,(w,s) in enumerate(zip(wt,sg)):
        if w==s: continue
        key='r'+w+':d'+revcom(s)+','+str(i+1)
        score*=mm_scores[key]
    score*=pam_scores.get(pam2,1.0)
    return score

# verify: perfect match + GG
t='GAGTCCGAGCAGAAGAAGAA'
assert abs(cfd_score(t,t,'GG')-1.0)<1e-9
# CRISPOR documented example (Doench): guide vs off should give known product; just check monotonicity
off=list(t); off[19]='C'; off=''.join(off)   # PAM-proximal mismatch -> low score
off2=list(t); off2[0]='C'; off2=''.join(off2) # distal mismatch -> high score
print("perfect:",cfd_score(t,t,'GG'))
print("PAM-proximal mm (pos20):", round(cfd_score(t,off,'GG'),4))
print("distal mm (pos1):", round(cfd_score(t,off2,'GG'),4))
print("=> distal should score higher than proximal:", cfd_score(t,off2,'GG')>cfd_score(t,off,'GG'))

content.thinking:
CFD is correct — PAM-proximal mismatches are more penalized than distal, as expected biologically. Now let me also define the MIT specificity score. The MIT off-target score (Hsu 2013) for an individual off-target site uses the formula with the experimentally-determined position weights W. The aggregate MIT specificity score for a guide is:

MIT_spec = 100 / (100 + sum over off-targets of MIT_single_score)

Let me define the single-off-target MIT score (Hsu 2013):
score = 1 / ( (1 + mean_pairwise_distance_factor...) ) ... the formula:

For a single off-target with mismatches at positions, the MIT score:
S = prod over mismatches (1 - W[position]) * 1/((((19-d_avg)/19)*4 + 1)) * 1/(n_mm^2)

where W is the position-specific weight vector (20 values), d_avg is the mean distance between consecutive mismatches, n_mm is number of mismatches.

{"stdout":"def calcHitScore(string1,string2):
    " see 'Scores of single hits' on http://crispr.mit.edu/about "
    # The Patrick Hsu weighting scheme
    # S. aureus requires 21bp long guides. We fudge by using only last 20bp
    matrixStart = 0
    maxDist = 19

    assert(string1[0].isupper())
    assert(len(string1)==len(string2))
    #for nmCas9 and a few others with longer guides, we limit ourselves to 20bp
    if len(string1)>20:
        string1 = string1[-20:]
        string2 = string2[-20:]
    # for 19bp guides, we fudge a little, but first pos has no weight anyways
    elif len(string1)==19:
        string1 = "A"+string1
        string2 = "A"+string2
    # for shorter guides, I'm not sure if this score makes sense anymore, we force things
    elif len(string1)<19:
        matrixStart = 20-len(string1)
        maxDist = len(string1)-1

    assert(len(string1)==len(string2))

    dists = [] # distances between mismatches, for part 2
    mmCount = 0 # number of mismatches, for part 3
    lastMmPos = None # position of last mismatch, used to calculate distance

    score1 = 1.0
    for pos in range(matrixStart, len(string1)):
        if string1[pos]!=string2[pos]:
            mmCount+=1
            if lastMmPos!=None:
                dists.append(pos-lastMmPos)
            score1 *= 1-hitScoreM[pos]
            lastMmPos = pos
    # 2nd part of the score
    if mmCount<2: # special case, not shown in the paper
        score2 = 1.0
    else:
        avgDist = sum(dists)/len(dists)
        score2 = 1.0 / (((maxDist-avgDist)/float(maxDist)) * 4 + 1)
    # 3rd part of the score
    if mmCount==0: # special case, not shown in the paper
        score3 = 1.0
    else:
        score3 = 1.0 / (mmCount**2)

    score = score1 * score2 * score3 * 100
    return score

def calcMitGuideScore(hitSum):
    """ Sguide defined on http://crispr.mit.edu/about
    Input is the sum of all off-target hit scores. Returns the specificity of the guide.
    """
    score = 100 / (100+hitSum)
    score = int(round(score*100))
    return score

def calcCfdGuideScore(hitSum):
    " suggested by Nicholas Parkinson "
    norm_score = 100.* 100.0 / (hitSum)
    return norm_score

input.code:
hitScoreM=[0,0,0.014,0,0,0.395,0.317,0,0.389,0.079,0.445,0.508,0.613,0.851,0.732,0.828,0.615,0.804,0.685,0.583]

def mit_hit_score(s1,s2):
    """MIT/Hsu single-hit score (0-100). s1=guide, s2=offtarget, both 20nt."""
    s1=s1.upper(); s2=s2.upper()
    assert len(s1)==len(s2)==20
    dists=[]; mmCount=0; lastMm=None; score1=1.0; maxDist=19
    for pos in range(20):
        if s1[pos]!=s2[pos]:
            mmCount+=1
            if lastMm is not None: dists.append(pos-lastMm)
            score1*=1-hitScoreM[pos]; lastMm=pos
    if mmCount<2: score2=1.0
    else:
        avgDist=sum(dists)/len(dists)
        score2=1.0/(((maxDist-avgDist)/maxDist)*4+1)
    score3=1.0 if mmCount==0 else 1.0/(mmCount**2)
    return score1*score2*score3*100

def mit_guide_specificity(hitsum):
    return int(round(100/(100+hitsum)*100))   # 0-100, higher=more specific

# Compute CFD and MIT hit scores for every hit (vectorized-ish via apply)
def score_row(r):
    g, o, pam, nmm = r['guide'], r['ot_20'], r['ot_pam'], r['n_mm']
    if nmm==0:
        return 1.0, 100.0
    return cfd_score(g,o,pam[-2:]), mit_hit_score(g,o)
scores = off.apply(score_row, axis=1, result_type='expand')
off['cfd']=scores[0]; off['mit_hit']=scores[1]
print("scored. sample high-CFD off-targets (nmm>=1):")
print(off[off['n_mm']>=1].nlargest(5,'cfd')[['guide','ot_20','ot_pam','n_mm','cfd','mit_hit','chrom']].to_string())

# Separate on-target (the intended cut) from true off-targets.
# On-target = 0-mm hit matching the guide. For guides with multiple 0-mm, all but one are off-targets (multi-cut).
off['is_perfect'] = off['n_mm']==0
# Aggregate per guide
def agg_guide(sub):
    perf = sub['is_perfect'].sum()
    ot = sub[~((sub['is_perfect']) & (sub.index==sub[sub['is_perfect']].index.min()))]  # exclude one on-target
    # simpler: off-targets = all hits except exactly one perfect on-target
    n_total=len(sub)
    n_ot = n_total-1 if perf>=1 else n_total
    return pd.Series(dict(
        n_perfect_sites=perf,
        ot_0mm = max(perf-1,0),
        ot_1mm=(sub['n_mm']==1).sum(),
        ot_2mm=(sub['n_mm']==2).sum(),
        ot_3mm=(sub['n_mm']==3).sum(),
        n_offtarget=n_ot,
        cfd_ot_sum=sub.loc[~sub['is_perfect'],'cfd'].sum() + max(perf-1,0)*1.0,
        max_ot_cfd=sub.loc[~sub['is_perfect'],'cfd'].max() if (~sub['is_perfect']).any() else (1.0 if perf>1 else 0.0),
        mit_hitsum=sub.loc[~sub['is_perfect'],'mit_hit'].sum() + max(perf-1,0)*100.0,
    ))
gagg = off.groupby('guide').apply(agg_guide).reset_index()
print("\naggregated guides:", len(gagg))
print(gagg.head(3).to_string())

import gzip, re
# Build interval lists per chromosome for CDS and exon features. This is ~1.6M lines; do it once.
cds_by_chrom={}   # chrom -> list of (start,end,gene)
exon_by_chrom={}
pat_gn=re.compile(r'gene_name "([^"]+)"')
n=0
with gzip.open('annot.gtf.gz','rt') as f:
    for line in f:
        if line[0]=='#': continue
        p=line.split('\t')
        feat=p[2]
        if feat not in ('CDS','exon'): continue
        chrom=p[0]; start=int(p[3]); end=int(p[4])
        m=pat_gn.search(p[8]); gene=m.group(1) if m else ''
        d = cds_by_chrom if feat=='CDS' else exon_by_chrom
        d.setdefault(chrom,[]).append((start,end,gene))
        n+=1
print("parsed features:", n)
print("CDS chroms:", len(cds_by_chrom), "| total CDS intervals:", sum(len(v) for v in cds_by_chrom.values()))
print("exon chroms:", len(exon_by_chrom), "| total exon intervals:", sum(len(v) for v in exon_by_chrom.values()))

import numpy as np
from bisect import bisect_right
# Build sorted start arrays + running max-end for fast overlap query per chrom
def build_index(d):
    idx={}
    for c,ivs in d.items():
        ivs=sorted(ivs)
        starts=np.array([x[0] for x in ivs])
        ends=np.array([x[1] for x in ivs])
        genes=[x[2] for x in ivs]
        maxend=np.maximum.accumulate(ends)  # for pruning
        idx[c]=(starts,ends,genes,maxend)
    return idx
cds_idx=build_index(cds_by_chrom)
exon_idx=build_index(exon_by_chrom)

def overlaps(idx, chrom, pos, span_end):
    """does interval [pos, span_end] overlap any feature? return gene or None"""
    if chrom not in idx: return None
    starts,ends,genes,maxend=idx[chrom]
    # candidate intervals with start <= span_end
    hi=bisect_right(starts, span_end)
    if hi==0: return None
    # among [0,hi), find one with end>=pos. Scan back using maxend prune.
    lo=0
    # linear-ish but pruned: check where maxend>=pos
    # find earliest index where maxend>=pos
    left=np.searchsorted(maxend[:hi], pos, side='left')
    for j in range(left,hi):
        if ends[j]>=pos and starts[j]<=span_end:
            return genes[j]
    return None

# Off-target protospacer spans 20bp starting at 'pos' (Cas-OFFinder pos is 0-based start of 23mer on + strand)
# Use pos..pos+22 as the cut-region footprint.
ot = off[~off['is_perfect']].copy()
# also include duplicate perfect cuts as off-targets: mark extra perfect hits
# (handled in aggregation; here annotate all non-first-perfect too) — annotate ALL hits for completeness is cheap enough? 17k rows
def annotate(r):
    c=r['chrom']; p=int(r['pos']); e=p+22
    cg=overlaps(cds_idx,c,p,e)
    eg=overlaps(exon_idx,c,p,e) if cg is None else cg
    return pd.Series({'ot_in_CDS': cg is not None, 'ot_in_exon': eg is not None, 'ot_gene': eg or cg or ''})
ann = ot.apply(annotate, axis=1)
ot = pd.concat([ot, ann], axis=1)
print("annotated off-targets:", len(ot))
print("in CDS (coding):", ot['ot_in_CDS'].sum(), "| in exon:", ot['ot_in_exon'].sum(), "| intergenic/intronic:", (~ot['ot_in_exon']).sum())
print("\nHigh-risk = off-target in CDS with CFD>=0.2:")
hr = ot[(ot['ot_in_CDS']) & (ot['cfd']>=0.2)]
print("  count:", len(hr), "| affecting", hr['guide'].nunique(), "guides")

input.code:
# Merge on-target QC flags onto the off-target table for a combined verdict
ot_qc = pd.read_csv('guide_ontarget_qc.csv')[['sgRNA_id','ontarget_flag','flag_homopolymer','flag_GC_extreme','RS2_score','GC_pct','max_homopolymer']]
final = og2.merge(ot_qc, on='sgRNA_id', how='left')

# Paralog multi-cut: flag_multicut where the duplicate is a close paralog -> keep but annotate.
# Distinguish "disqualifying" off-target risk from paralog-duplication.
final['paralog_multicut'] = final['flag_multicut']   # all current multicut = perfect paralog dup
# Disqualifying = coding off-target OR very low specificity (NOT paralog dup alone)
final['disqualify_offtarget'] = final['flag_coding_offtarget'] | (final['MIT_specificity']<30)

# Final per-guide verdict: PASS unless on-target flag (homopolymer/GC) OR disqualifying off-target
final['guide_verdict'] = np.where(
    final['ontarget_flag'] | final['disqualify_offtarget'], 'FLAG', 'PASS')
print("Guide verdicts:", final['guide_verdict'].value_counts().to_dict())
print("  flagged for on-target only:", (final['ontarget_flag'] & ~final['disqualify_offtarget']).sum())
print("  flagged for off-target (coding/low-spec):", final['disqualify_offtarget'].sum())
print("  flagged for both:", (final['ontarget_flag'] & final['disqualify_offtarget']).sum())

# Acceptable guides per gene
acc = final.groupby('Kinome_Symbol')['guide_verdict'].apply(lambda s: (s=='PASS').sum())
print("\nAcceptable (PASS) guides per gene: min=%d"%acc.min())
print("  genes with 4 PASS:", (acc==4).sum())
print("  genes with 3 PASS:", (acc==3).sum())
print("  genes with 2 PASS:", (acc==2).sum())
print("  genes with <2 PASS:", (acc<2).sum())
print("\nGenes needing de-novo supplementation (<3 acceptable guides):")
need_supp = acc[acc<3]
print(need_supp.to_string() if len(need_supp) else "  none")

# Reload full Brunello to extract control + positive-control guides
bru = pd.read_csv('/tmp/brunello.txt', sep='\t')
bru_ctrl = bru[bru['Target Gene Symbol'].astype(str).str.contains('Non-Targeting', case=False, na=False)].copy()
print("Brunello non-targeting controls available:", len(bru_ctrl))

# 1) NON-TARGETING CONTROLS: use all 1000 from Brunello
ntc = bru_ctrl.rename(columns={'sgRNA Target Sequence':'sgRNA_sequence'})[['sgRNA_sequence']].copy()
ntc['sgRNA_id']=['NTC_%04d'%(i+1) for i in range(len(ntc))]
ntc['Kinome_Symbol']='NonTargeting'; ntc['Group']='control'; ntc['guide_type']='non_targeting_control'
ntc['PAM']=''; ntc['RS2_score']=np.nan

# 2) POSITIVE CONTROLS: core-essential genes (dropout positives) from Brunello
pan_essential = ['RPL3','RPL5','RPS11','RPS19','POLR2A','POLR2D','PCNA','EIF3B',
                 'PSMA1','PSMB2','PSMD1','SF3B1','RPL11','RPS3','KIF11','CDK1','PLK1']
bru_noctrl = bru[~bru['Target Gene Symbol'].astype(str).str.contains('Non-Targeting',case=False,na=False)]
pos = bru_noctrl[bru_noctrl['Target Gene Symbol'].isin(pan_essential)].copy()
pos = pos.rename(columns={'sgRNA Target Sequence':'sgRNA_sequence','Target Gene Symbol':'Kinome_Symbol',
                          'PAM Sequence':'PAM','Rule Set 2 score':'RS2_score'})
pos['sgRNA_id']=pos['Kinome_Symbol']+'_pos_'+pos.groupby('Kinome_Symbol').cumcount().add(1).astype(str)
pos['Group']='positive_essential_control'
pos['guide_type']=np.where(pos['Kinome_Symbol'].isin(['CDK1','PLK1']),'positive_control_essential_kinase','positive_control_essential')
print("Positive-control genes found:", sorted(pos['Kinome_Symbol'].unique()))
print("Positive-control guides:", len(pos))
print("  (missing from Brunello:", set(pan_essential)-set(pos['Kinome_Symbol']),")")

# Controls - align columns

# De-novo worklist: 4 absent genes + 33 genes with <3 PASS guides
absent = pd.DataFrame({'Kinome_Symbol':['GRK1','STK38','PRKY','CDK8'],
                       'reason':'absent_from_Brunello','n_pass_guides':0,'n_guides_needed':4})
kinome_master = pd.read_csv('kinome_genes.csv')

supp['reason']='low_quality_or_specificity'; supp['n_guides_needed']=3-supp['n_pass_guides']
worklist = pd.concat([absent, supp], ignore_index=True).merge(
    kinome_master[['Symbol','Group','Family','UniProtID','Entrez']], left_on='Kinome_Symbol', right_on='Symbol', how='left').drop(columns='Symbol')
worklist = worklist.sort_values(['n_pass_guides','Kinome_Symbol'])

ax=axes[1]

fl = fl[~bsmbi].reset_index(drop=True)

# --- Oligo design for BsmBI/Esp3I Golden-Gate into lentiGuide-Puro (Zhang lab) ---
# Standard GPP/Brunello oligo architecture (73-mer) for BsmBI cloning:
#   5' adapter (fwd primer + BsmBI + overhang CACCG) + 20nt spacer + 3' overhang + adapter (rev primer)
# Canonical Broad GPP pooled-synthesis design:
FWD_ADAPTER = "AGGCACTTGCTCGTACGACGCGTCTCACACCG"  # contains BsmBI (CGTCTC) -> leaves CACCG overhang
REV_ADAPTER = "GTTTAGAGACGTTAAGGTGCCGGGCCCACAT"  # BsmBI (rev, GAGACG) -> leaves AAAC overhang on bottom strand
# Note: for spacers not starting with G, the CACCG overhang supplies the +1 G (standard lentiGuide approach).

def build_oligo(spacer):
    return FWD_ADAPTER + spacer + REV_ADAPTER

fl['full_oligo'] = fl['sgRNA_sequence'].str.upper().apply(build_oligo)
fl['oligo_len'] = fl['full_oligo'].str.len()
print("\nOligo length:", fl['oligo_len'].unique(), "nt (uniform)")

# Verify: exactly the designed BsmBI sites present (2 per oligo: one in each adapter), none extra from spacer
def count_bsmbi(s):
    return s.count('CGTCTC')+s.count('GAGACG')
fl['n_bsmbi_sites']=fl['full_oligo'].apply(count_bsmbi)
print("BsmBI sites per oligo:", fl['n_bsmbi_sites'].value_counts().to_dict())

content.text: Now let me write the construction protocol document.

input.new_string:
# Kinome CRISPR-KO Library — Construction Protocol

**Library:** 3,129 sgRNAs (2,072 kinase-targeting + 989 non-targeting + 68 positive-control)
**Vector:** lentiGuide-Puro (Addgene #52963) or lentiCRISPRv2 (Addgene #52961)
**Cloning enzyme:** Esp3I / BsmBI (Golden Gate)
**Design basis:** Brunello (Doench et al. 2016), subset to the human protein kinome.

---

## 1. Oligo pool design

Each 83-nt oligo:

```
5'-[AGGCACTTGCTCGTACGACGCGTCTCACACCG]-[20-nt spacer]-[GTTTAGAGACGTTAAGGTGCCGGGCCCACAT]-3'
      fwd amplification + BsmBI + CACCG overhang        BsmBI + AAAC overhang + rev amplification
```

- The two BsmBI recognition sites (`CGTCTC` forward, `GAGACG` reverse) face **inward**; digestion excises the adapters and leaves 4-nt overhangs (`CACCG` / `AAAC`) complementary to BsmBI-linearized lentiGuide-Puro.
- The `CACCG` overhang supplies the U6 **+1 G** required for Pol III transcription, so spacers need not begin with G.
- **9 targeting spacers begin with `TCTC`**, which fuses with the `...CACCG` adapter tail to create a spurious third BsmBI site. These are flagged `needs_separate_cloning=True` in `oligo_pool_synthesis.csv`. Clone them in a separate BbsI-based reaction (or individually by annealed-oligo cloning). Every affected gene retains ≥2 other guides, so dropping them from the main pool does not reduce any gene below 2 guides.
- Pool is otherwise free of internal BsmBI sites (8 non-targeting controls with internal BsmBI were removed during design).

Order `oligo_pool_synthesis.csv` / `oligo_order.txt` as an oligonucleotide pool (Twist Bioscience, GenScript, or CustomArray; ~3,129 × 83-mer).

## 2. Pool amplification (emulsion/low-cycle PCR)

- Resuspend pool to 1 ng/µL. Amplify with primers matching the outer adapters.
- Use a **minimal cycle number (≤15–18 cycles)** and split across ≥8 parallel 50-µL reactions to limit PCR skew/jackpotting.
- Gel-verify a single band; PCR-purify (column or 1.0× AMPure XP).

## 3. Golden-Gate assembly (BsmBI)

Per 20-µL reaction:
| Component | Amount |
|---|---|
| lentiGuide-Puro (pre-dephosphorylated or single-tube GG) | 100 ng |
| Amplified insert pool | molar ratio insert:vector ≈ 2:1 |
| Esp3I (BsmBI) | 1 µL (NEB) |
| T4 DNA Ligase | 1 µL |
| T4 Ligase Buffer (10×) | 2 µL |

Thermocycle: 30–50 cycles of (37 °C 5 min → 16 °C 5 min), then 37 °C 15 min, 65 °C 15 min (heat-kill). Purify by isopropanol precipitation or 0.7× AMPure; elute in 10 µL.

## 4. Transformation & representation

- Electroporate into **Endura** or **Lucigenic** electrocompetent *E. coli* (≥10¹⁰ cfu/µg). Use enough reactions to obtain **≥300× representation**.
- **Target: ≥ 938,700 independent colonies** (3,129 guides × 300×). Plate serial dilutions to count cfu and confirm coverage **before** scraping.
- Scrape all colonies from large bioassay plates; midi/maxi-prep plasmid DNA.

## 5. Coverage QC

- Deep-sequence the plasmid pool (amplicon NGS of the spacer region) at ≥500 reads/guide.
- Acceptance: **>90–95 % of guides detected**, **skew ratio (90th/10th percentile) < 10**, Gini index < 0.1. Re-clone if skew or dropout is excessive.

## 6. Lentivirus production

- Co-transfect HEK293T/293FT with the library plasmid + psPAX2 (gag-pol) + pMD2.G (VSV-G) at standard ratios (4:3:1 or 4:2:1).
- Harvest viral supernatant at 48 h and 72 h; 0.45-µm filter; concentrate if needed; aliquot and store at −80 °C.
- **Titer** on the target cell line (functional titer by puromycin-resistant colony counting at serial dilutions) to determine the volume giving MOI ≈ 0.3.

## Key parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Unique sgRNAs | 3,129 |
| Oligo length | 83 nt |
| Cloning enzyme | Esp3I / BsmBI |
| Backbone | lentiGuide-Puro / lentiCRISPRv2 |
| Cloning representation | ≥ 300× (≥ 938,700 colonies) |
| Separate-cloning guides | 9 (TCTC-leading, BbsI) |

content.text: **Step 7 — transduction and sequencing depth planning.** Building the full scale calculator: cells for ≥500× representation at MOI 0.3, experimental design (conditions × replicates × timepoints), and NGS read budget.

input.code:
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
N = 3129                      # guides in library
REP = 500                    # fold representation to maintain
MOI = 0.3                    # low MOI -> ~1 integration/cell (Poisson)
# fraction of cells infected at MOI 0.3 (single+ integrations) = 1 - e^-MOI
frac_infected = 1 - np.exp(-MOI)
# fraction with >=1 integration among ALL cells = frac_infected; puro selects these.
print(f"Library guides N = {N:,}")
print(f"MOI = {MOI} -> fraction of cells transduced (>=1 integration) = {frac_infected:.3f}")

# --- Transduction scale ---
cells_to_maintain = N*REP                          # transduced cells to keep at each passage
cells_to_infect_total = cells_to_maintain/frac_infected   # total cells to seed for infection (only ~26% get a guide)
print(f"\n=== TRANSDUCTION SCALE (per infection replicate) ===")
print(f"Transduced cells to maintain at {REP}x: {cells_to_maintain:,.0f}")
print(f"Total cells to seed at MOI {MOI} (since only {frac_infected:.0%} transduced): {cells_to_infect_total:,.0f}")
print(f"After puromycin selection, carry forward >= {cells_to_maintain:,.0f} cells at every passage")

# --- Experimental design ---
conditions = {'T0_reference':1, 'dropout_untreated':1, 'treatment_drugA':1}  # example
n_conditions = 3
n_replicates = 3
designs = n_conditions*n_replicates
print(f"\n=== EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN ===")
print(f"Conditions: {n_conditions} (T0 reference, untreated dropout endpoint, treatment arm)")
print(f"Replicates: {n_replicates} independent infections per condition")
print(f"Total samples (libraries to sequence): {designs}")
# cells needed total
total_cells_all = cells_to_infect_total*n_replicates
print(f"Total cells to transduce across {n_replicates} reps: {total_cells_all:,.0f} (~{total_cells_all/1e6:.0f}e6 cells)")

print(f"\nRecommendation: a single NovaSeq SP or NextSeq 2000 P3 lane (~0.8-1.1B reads) covers all {designs} samples at 1000x with headroom;")

# Build sequencing_depth_plan.csv — scenarios across representation choices
scenarios=[]
for rep in [300,500,1000]:
    for rpg in [300,500,1000]:
        frac=1-np.exp(-0.3)
        maintain=N*rep
        seed=maintain/frac
        gdna_ng=maintain*6.6/1000
        reads_samp=N*rpg
        reads_tot=reads_samp*9
        scenarios.append(dict(
            representation_x=rep, reads_per_guide=rpg,
            cells_maintain=int(maintain), cells_seed_MOI0p3=int(seed),
            gDNA_ng_per_sample=round(gdna_ng,0), PCR_wells_200ng=int(np.ceil(gdna_ng/200)),
            reads_per_sample=int(reads_samp), total_reads_9samples=int(reads_tot),
            pct_NovaSeq_SP=round(reads_tot/0.8e9*100,2)))
depth=pd.DataFrame(scenarios)
depth.to_csv('sequencing_depth_plan.csv', index=False)
print("Saved sequencing_depth_plan.csv")
print(depth.to_string(index=False))

import matplotlib; matplotlib.use('Agg'); import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(13,5))
# (a) cells needed vs representation
reps=np.array([100,200,300,500,750,1000])
frac=1-np.exp(-0.3)
maintain=N*reps; seed=maintain/frac
ax=axes[0]
ax.plot(reps, maintain/1e6, 'o-', color='#2c7fb8', label='transduced cells to maintain')
ax.plot(reps, seed/1e6, 's--', color='#d95f0e', label='total cells to seed (MOI 0.3)')
ax.axvline(500, color='green', ls=':', lw=1.5, label='recommended 500x')
ax.set_xlabel('Fold representation (×)'); ax.set_ylabel('Cells (millions)')
ax.set_title(f'(a) Cell numbers per replicate (N={N:,} guides)'); ax.legend(fontsize=8)
# (b) total reads vs reads/guide for 9 samples
rpg=np.array([100,200,300,500,750,1000,1500])
tot=N*rpg*9
ax=axes[1]
ax.plot(rpg, tot/1e6, 'o-', color='#756bb1')
ax.axhline(0.8e9/1e6, color='red', ls='--', lw=1, label='NovaSeq SP capacity (~800M)')
ax.axvline(1000, color='green', ls=':', lw=1.5, label='recommended 1000 reads/guide')
ax.set_xlabel('Target reads per guide'); ax.set_ylabel('Total reads, 9 samples (millions)')
ax.set_title('(b) Sequencing budget'); ax.legend(fontsize=8)
for ax in axes:
    for s in ['top','right']: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)
fig.suptitle('Transduction & sequencing depth planning — kinome KO screen', y=1.01, fontsize=12)
fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig('coverage_vs_cells.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
print("saved figure")

input.code:
grp_counts = kin['Group'].value_counts().to_dict() grp_rows = "".join(f"{g}{c}" for g,c in sorted(grp_counts.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1])) html = f""" Kinome CRISPR-KO Screen — Design Report

Genome-wide CRISPR Knockout Screen — Human Kinome

Library design, guide selection, off-target prediction, construction & sequencing plan · SpCas9 (NGG) · Brunello-based

{stats['n_kinases']}
kinase genes ({stats['n_groups']} Manning groups)
{stats['n_final_lib']:,}
sgRNAs in final library
{stats['n_pool']:,}
oligos in synthesis pool
{stats['med_mit']}
median MIT specificity
{stats['n_highrisk']}
high-risk off-target guides

1. Kinome definition

The target gene set is the human protein kinome from the KinHub / Manning classification, enriched with current UniProt gene symbols and NCBI Entrez IDs. After collapsing dual-domain kinases (e.g. JAK1/2, GSK3, OBSCN) to the gene level, the set contains {stats['n_kinases']} unique kinase genes across {stats['n_groups']} groups.

{grp_rows}
Kinase groupGenes

2. Guide selection (Brunello)

Guides were drawn from the Brunello genome-wide human CRISPR-KO library (Doench et al., Nat Biotechnol 2016; 76,441 targeting sgRNAs + 1,000 non-targeting controls, 4 guides/gene, optimized with Azimuth Rule Set 2 on-target and CFD off-target scoring). Mapping the kinome onto Brunello by symbol + curated aliases:

  • {stats['n_in_brunello']} / {stats['n_kinases']} kinases present in Brunello with exactly 4 guides each → {stats['n_targeting']:,} targeting sgRNAs.
  • {stats['n_absent']} absent from Brunello (GRK1, STK38, PRKY, CDK8) → flagged for de-novo design.
  • A false alias match (RP1, retinitis-pigmentosa gene, Entrez 6101) incorrectly mapping to kinase STK19/WHR1 was removed.

3. On-target quality

Every selected guide was screened for GC content, poly-T (TTTT) Pol-III terminators, homopolymer runs, and internal BsmBI/Esp3I cloning sites, alongside its Rule Set 2 score. Brunello guides are pre-optimized, so quality is high: median Rule Set 2 = {stats['med_rs2']}, no TTTT terminators, no internal BsmBI sites, GC all within 20–80 %. The only on-target flags are 69 guides with a homopolymer run ≥5 bp and 10 with extreme GC; no gene loses more than 2 guides to on-target flags.

4. Genome-wide off-target prediction

All 2,072 spacers were aligned genome-wide against GRCh38 (Ensembl release 110 primary assembly) with Cas-OFFinder (NGG PAM, ≤3 mismatches), yielding 17,584 candidate off-target sites. Each was scored with the CFD (Doench 2016) and MIT/Hsu specificity metrics, and annotated against Ensembl CDS/exon intervals.

  • Median aggregate MIT specificity = {stats['med_mit']} (max 100); the library is highly specific overall.
  • {stats['n_highrisk']} guides ({100*stats['n_highrisk']/stats['n_targeting']:.1f}%) flagged high-risk: {stats['n_coding_ot']} with a high-CFD (≥0.2) off-target in a coding exon, {stats['n_multicut']} cutting multiple perfect genomic sites, 3 with very low specificity.
  • All 4 SIK1 guides cut two perfect sites each — these are its near-identical paralog SIK1B (segmental duplication). They still knock out SIK1 and are retained with an annotation.

5. Final library & controls

The final library is {stats['n_final_lib']:,} sgRNAs:

ClasssgRNAsNotes
Kinase-targeting (PASS){stats['n_pass']:,}clean on- and off-target QC
Kinase-targeting (flagged, retained){stats['n_flag']}annotated; Brunello has no within-library alternate
Non-targeting controls{stats['n_ntc']:,}Brunello NTC set (negative/normalization)
Positive essential controls{stats['n_pos']}RPL/RPS, POLR2A, PCNA, proteasome, KIF11, SF3B1 + essential kinases CDK1, PLK1
Reselection logic. Because Brunello supplies exactly 4 fixed guides per gene, flagged guides cannot be swapped within the library; they are retained but annotated (guide_verdict, offtarget_highrisk) so they can be down-weighted in analysis. {stats['n_worklist']} genes ({stats['n_absent']} absent + {stats['n_worklist']-stats['n_absent']} with <3 acceptable guides) are placed on a de-novo worklist totaling {stats['n_worklist_guides']} custom guides (denovo_design_worklist.csv) for labs wanting ≥3 high-confidence guides per gene.

6. Library construction

Synthesis-ready 83-mer oligos carry inward-facing BsmBI/Esp3I sites with CACCG/AAAC overhangs for Golden-Gate cloning into lentiGuide-Puro (Addgene #52963) or lentiCRISPRv2. The CACCG overhang supplies the U6 +1 G, so spacers need not start with G.

  • Pool is internal-BsmBI-clean: 8 non-targeting controls containing BsmBI and 3 junction-conflict NTCs were removed → {stats['n_pool']:,} oligos.
  • {stats['n_sep_clone']} targeting guides begin with TCTC, creating a junction BsmBI site; flagged needs_separate_cloning for a parallel BbsI reaction. Each affected gene keeps ≥2 other guides.
  • Cloning representation target ≥300× → ≈939,000 independent colonies; pool QC by NGS (>90–95 % detected, skew <10).

Full step-by-step protocol: construction_protocol.md; oligo sequences: oligo_pool_synthesis.csv / oligo_order.txt.

7. Transduction & sequencing depth

Recommended operating point: MOI ≈ 0.3 (≈26 % of cells transduced, ~1 integration/cell) and ≥500× representation maintained at every passage.

ParameterValue (500× / MOI 0.3 / 1,000 reads per guide)
Transduced cells to maintain per replicate1,564,500
Total cells to seed for infection≈ 6.0 million
Example design3 conditions × 3 replicates = 9 samples
gDNA input per readout PCR≈ 10.3 µg (~52 wells × 200 ng)
Reads per sample3,129,000
Total reads (9 samples)≈ 28 million (~3.5 % of a NovaSeq SP flow cell; ~2.1 Gb)

Because the library is small (~3.1k guides), one NovaSeq SP or NextSeq 2000 P3 lane covers all samples at 1,000 reads/guide with large headroom for multiplexing additional screens. Scenario matrix: sequencing_depth_plan.csv.

8. Methods & provenance

  • Kinome: KinHub (kinhub.org) Manning classification; symbols/Entrez via UniProt REST.
  • Guides: Broad GPP Brunello library contents (Doench et al. 2016).
  • Off-target search: Cas-OFFinder v2.4.1 (Bae et al. 2014), GRCh38 Ensembl r110, NGG, ≤3 mm, CPU/POCL.
  • Scoring: CFD (Doench et al. 2016) and MIT/Hsu (Hsu et al. 2013) matrices from the CRISPOR reference implementation.
  • Annotation: Ensembl r110 GTF (CDS + exon intervals).

Assumptions: human, SpCas9/NGG PAM, 4 guides/gene, MOI 0.3. Adjust representation and reads/guide via the depth-plan scenarios.

""" open('design_report.html','w').write(html) print("Wrote design_report.html:", len(html), "chars")

# Human Kinome CRISPR-KO Screen — Design Package

Genome-wide SpCas9 knockout library targeting the human protein kinome (522 kinases),
built on the Brunello library with genome-wide off-target re-analysis, controls, and
full construction + sequencing plans.

## Deliverables

### Report
- **design_report.html** — self-contained report with all six figures and every parameter.

### Gene set & library tables
- **kinome_genes.csv** — 522 kinase genes (Manning group/family, UniProt, Entrez, Brunello coverage, de-novo flag).
- **kinase_guides_brunello.csv** — 2,072 selected Brunello guides (4/gene) with coordinates and Rule Set 2 scores.
- **final_kinome_library.csv** — complete 3,140-sgRNA library (targeting + controls) with QC verdicts. *(>1 MB)*
- **oligo_pool_synthesis.csv** — 3,129 synthesis-ready 83-mer oligos with BsmBI adapters. *(>1 MB)*
- **oligo_order.txt** — id⇥oligo list for vendor ordering.

### QC & off-target
- **guide_ontarget_qc.csv** — per-guide GC, poly-T, homopolymer, BsmBI, Rule Set 2 flags.
- **offtarget_report.csv** — per-guide CFD/MIT specificity, mismatch-class counts, coding off-target risk.
- **offtarget_coding_hits.csv** — the 135 high-CFD coding off-target sites (guide, gene, locus).
- **denovo_design_worklist.csv** — 37 genes (53 guides) needing custom design.

### Plans
- **construction_protocol.md** — oligo architecture, Golden-Gate cloning, transformation, QC.
- **sequencing_depth_plan.csv** — representation × reads/guide scenario matrix.

### Figures
- kinome_groups.png · guides_per_gene.png · ontarget_score_distributions.png
- offtarget_specificity.png · library_composition.png · coverage_vs_cells.png

## Headline numbers
| | |
|---|---|
| Kinase genes targeted | 522 (518 in Brunello, 4 de-novo) |
| Targeting sgRNAs | 2,072 (4/gene) |
| Final library | 3,140 sgRNAs (2,072 targeting + 1,000 NTC + 68 positive) |
| Synthesis pool | 3,129 oligos (83-mer, BsmBI) |
| Median MIT specificity | 97 |
| High-risk off-target guides | 128 (6.2%) — retained, annotated |
| Cloning representation | ≥300× (≈939k colonies) |
| Screen representation | ≥500× (1.56M cells/replicate, MOI 0.3) |
| Sequencing | ~28M reads for 9 samples @1,000 reads/guide (~3.5% NovaSeq SP) |

## Methods
KinHub/Manning kinome · Brunello (Doench 2016) · Cas-OFFinder v2.4.1 vs GRCh38
(Ensembl r110), ≤3 mismatches · CFD (Doench 2016) + MIT/Hsu (2013) scoring ·
Ensembl r110 CDS/exon annotation.

content.text:
All 8 steps complete. Here's the summary.

## Human Kinome CRISPR-KO Screen — Design Complete

I designed a genome-wide SpCas9 knockout library targeting the entire human protein kinome, anchored on the published **Brunello** library with genuine genome-wide off-target re-analysis on top.

### What was built

**Kinome (522 genes).** Canonical KinHub/Manning classification across 10 kinase groups, enriched with current UniProt symbols and Entrez IDs.

![Kinome by group]({{artifact:e5ef8d6e-0563-4af4-baa4-b9b5444cc01b}})

**Guide selection.** 518/522 kinases are in Brunello with exactly 4 optimized guides each → **2,072 targeting sgRNAs**. Four (GRK1, STK38, PRKY, CDK8) are genuinely absent and flagged for de-novo design. I also caught and removed a false alias match (Brunello's `RP1` = retinitis-pigmentosa gene, not kinase STK19).

**On-target QC.** Brunello guides are pre-optimized — median Rule Set 2 = 0.67, zero TTTT terminators, zero internal BsmBI sites, GC all within 20–80%. Only 79 guides flagged (homopolymer/GC), max 2 per gene.

**Genome-wide off-target prediction.** All 2,072 spacers aligned against GRCh38 (Ensembl r110) with **Cas-OFFinder** (NGG, ≤3 mismatches) → 17,584 candidate sites, each scored with **CFD** and **MIT/Hsu** matrices and annotated against coding exons. Median MIT specificity **97**. **128 guides (6.2%)** are high-risk: 114 with a high-CFD off-target in a *coding* exon, 13 multi-cutting, 3 very-low-specificity. Notably, all 4 SIK1 guides cut its near-identical paralog SIK1B — a biological duplication, not a design flaw, so they're retained with a note.

![Off-target analysis]({{artifact:22b2b8d6-89ae-4f92-aece-e39884e58d34}})

**Final library — 3,140 sgRNAs:** 2,072 kinase-targeting (1,881 clean + 191 flagged-but-retained) + 1,000 non-targeting controls + 68 positive essential controls (ribosomal/proteasome/POLR2A/KIF11/SF3B1 plus essential kinases CDK1, PLK1). Because Brunello provides fixed guides with no alternates, flagged guides are annotated rather than swapped; a **37-gene de-novo worklist** (53 custom guides) documents where ≥3 high-confidence guides would require custom design.

**Construction.** A 3,129-oligo synthesis pool (83-mer, BsmBI/Esp3I Golden-Gate for lentiGuide-Puro). I removed 8 NTCs containing internal BsmBI sites and 3 junction-conflict NTCs, and flagged 9 `TCTC`-leading targeting guides for separate BbsI cloning (each affected gene keeps ≥2 other guides). Cloning target ≥300× ≈ 939,000 colonies.

**Transduction & sequencing.** At MOI 0.3 / ≥500× representation: maintain 1.56M transduced cells per replicate (seed ~6M), ~10.3 µg gDNA per readout PCR. For a 3-condition × 3-replicate design at 1,000 reads/guide, the whole screen is **~28M reads — about 3.5% of a NovaSeq SP flow cell.**

![Coverage vs cells]({{artifact:8ea24e63-98f2-4f93-ab2a-4d286e3588d4}})

### Key deliverables
- [design_report.html](design_report.html) — self-contained report, all 6 figures + every parameter
- [README.md](README.md) — full file index and headline numbers
- [kinome_genes.csv](kinome_genes.csv) — 522 kinases with coverage/de-novo flags
- [kinase_guides_brunello.csv](kinase_guides_brunello.csv) — 2,072 selected guides with coordinates
- [guide_ontarget_qc.csv](guide_ontarget_qc.csv) · [offtarget_report.csv](offtarget_report.csv) · [offtarget_coding_hits.csv](offtarget_coding_hits.csv) — QC and off-target detail
- [denovo_design_worklist.csv](denovo_design_worklist.csv) — 37 genes / 53 guides for custom design
- [construction_protocol.md](construction_protocol.md) · [sequencing_depth_plan.csv](sequencing_depth_plan.csv) — wet-lab plans
- [oligo_order.txt](oligo_order.txt) — vendor-ready oligo list

Per your instruction, the two files larger than ~1 MB — `final_kinome_library.csv` (the complete 3,140-guide annotated library) and `oligo_pool_synthesis.csv` (the 3,129-oligo synthesis table) — are referenced by name rather than linked; both are saved as artifacts.

One assumption worth confirming: I used a **3-condition × 3-replicate** design as the sequencing-budget example. If your actual screen has a different number of arms, timepoints, or replicates, the `sequencing_depth_plan.csv` scenarios scale linearly — tell me the design and I'll recompute the exact cell and read numbers.

root_frame.name: CRISPR Kinase Screen Guide Selection
root_frame.task_summary: Identify and evaluate optimal sgRNA guides for all human kinases (~500 genes), selecting high-efficiency targets with minimal off-target potential using computational tools like Cas-OFFinder or CRISPOR.

artifacts.extracted_code:
import pandas as pd
import requests
import io
import time
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Load KinHub HTML and parse kinase table
tables = pd.read_html('http://www.kinhub.org/kinases.html')
kh = tables[0]
kh.columns = ['xName','Manning_Name','HGNC','Kinase_Name','Group','Family','SubFamily','UniProtID']
kh = kh.fillna('')
for c in kh.columns:
    kh[c] = kh[c].astype(str).str.strip()

# Fetch UniProt data for all kinase UniProt IDs
uids = sorted(set(kh['UniProtID'].tolist()) - {''})

def fetch_batch(batch):
    q = " OR ".join(f"accession:{a}" for a in batch)
    url = "https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/search"
    params = {"query": q, "format":"tsv", "size":500,
              "fields":"accession,gene_primary,gene_synonym,xref_geneid,protein_name"}
    r = requests.get(url, params=params, timeout=60); r.raise_for_status()
    return pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(r.text), sep="\t")

frames = []
for i in range(0, len(uids), 80):
    b = uids[i:i+80]
    frames.append(fetch_batch(b)); time.sleep(0.3)
up = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)

up = up.rename(columns={'Entry':'UniProtID','Gene Names (primary)':'Gene_primary',
                        'Gene Names (synonym)':'Synonyms','GeneID':'Entrez','Protein names':'Protein'})
up['Entrez'] = up['Entrez'].astype(str).str.rstrip(';').str.split(';').str[0].replace('nan','')
up['Gene_primary'] = up['Gene_primary'].fillna('')

m = kh.merge(up[['UniProtID','Gene_primary','Synonyms','Entrez']], on='UniProtID', how='left')
m['Symbol'] = m['Gene_primary'].where(m['Gene_primary'].astype(bool), m['HGNC'])
m['Symbol'] = m['Symbol'].where(m['Symbol'].astype(bool), m['Manning_Name'])

kinome = (m.sort_values('Symbol')
            .groupby('Symbol', as_index=False)
            .agg({'Manning_Name':'first','Group':'first','Family':'first','SubFamily':'first',
                  'UniProtID':'first','Entrez':'first','Synonyms':'first','Kinase_Name':'first'}))

kinome['Entrez'] = kinome['Entrez'].replace('None','')
kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol']=='GTF2F1','Entrez'] = '2962'
kinome = kinome[['Symbol','Manning_Name','Group','Family','SubFamily','UniProtID','Entrez','Synonyms','Kinase_Name']]
kinome = kinome.sort_values(['Group','Family','Symbol']).reset_index(drop=True)

# Load Brunello library
bru = pd.read_csv('https://www.addgene.org/static/cms/filer_public/8b/4c/8b4c89d9-eac1-44b2-bb2f-8fea95672705/broadgpp-brunello-library-contents.txt', sep='\t')

ctrl_mask = bru['Target Gene Symbol'].astype(str).str.contains('Control|Non-Targeting|NonTargeting', case=False, na=False) | bru['Target Gene ID'].astype(str).str.contains('Control',case=False,na=False)
bt = bru[~ctrl_mask].copy()
bt['Target Gene ID'] = bt['Target Gene ID'].astype(str)

kin_syms = set(kinome['Symbol'])
kin_entrez = set(kinome['Entrez'].astype(str)) - {''}

bt_sym = bt['Target Gene Symbol'].isin(kin_syms)
bt_ent = bt['Target Gene ID'].isin(kin_entrez)
matched = bt_sym | bt_ent

found_by_sym = set(bt.loc[bt_sym,'Target Gene Symbol']) & kin_syms
found_entrez = set(bt.loc[bt_ent,'Target Gene ID'])
kinome['found'] = False
kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol'].isin(found_by_sym),'found'] = True
kinome.loc[kinome['Entrez'].astype(str).isin(found_entrez),'found'] = True

missing = kinome[~kinome['found']]

alias_map = {}
for _,r in missing.iterrows():
    syns = str(r['Synonyms']).replace('nan','').split()
    for s in syns:
        alias_map[s.upper()] = r['Symbol']
    alias_map[r['Manning_Name'].upper()] = r['Symbol']

bt_upper = bt['Target Gene Symbol'].str.upper()
alias_hits = bt_upper.isin(alias_map.keys())
found_aliases = bt.loc[alias_hits,'Target Gene Symbol'].unique()
for bsym in found_aliases:
    kinsym = alias_map[bsym.upper()]
    kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol']==kinsym,'found'] = True

bru2kin = {}
for s in kin_syms:
    bru2kin[s] = s
for bsym in found_aliases:
    bru2kin[bsym] = alias_map[bsym.upper()]
bru2kin['PRPF4B'] = 'PRP4K'

kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol']=='PRP4K','found'] = True

sel = bt[bt['Target Gene Symbol'].isin(bru2kin.keys())].copy()
sel['Kinome_Symbol'] = sel['Target Gene Symbol'].map(bru2kin)
sel = sel.merge(kinome[['Symbol','Group','Family']], left_on='Kinome_Symbol', right_on='Symbol', how='left').drop(columns='Symbol')

sel = sel[~((sel['Kinome_Symbol']=='WHR1') & (sel['Target Gene Symbol']=='RP1'))].copy()
del bru2kin['RP1']

denovo = ['GRK1','STK38','PRKY','CDK8']
kinome['found'] = ~kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)
kinome['needs_denovo_design'] = kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)
kinome.to_csv('kinome_genes.csv', index=False)

import pandas as pd
import requests
import io
import time

# Load kinome genes from saved artifact
kinome = pd.read_csv("{{artifact:cd56c267-e072-4096-a342-d491772117bf}}")
kinome['Entrez'] = kinome['Entrez'].astype(str)

# Download Brunello library
import urllib.request
url = "https://media.addgene.org/cms/filer_public/8b/4c/8b4c89d9-eac1-44b2-bb2f-8fea95672705/broadgpp-brunello-library-contents.txt"
urllib.request.urlretrieve(url, '/tmp/brunello.txt')

bru = pd.read_csv('/tmp/brunello.txt', sep='\t')

# Filter controls
ctrl_mask = bru['Target Gene Symbol'].astype(str).str.contains('Control|Non-Targeting|NonTargeting', case=False, na=False) | bru['Target Gene ID'].astype(str).str.contains('Control',case=False,na=False)
bt = bru[~ctrl_mask].copy()
bt['Target Gene ID'] = bt['Target Gene ID'].astype(str)

kin_syms = set(kinome['Symbol'])
kin_entrez = set(kinome['Entrez'].astype(str)) - {''}

# 1) direct symbol match
bt_sym = bt['Target Gene Symbol'].isin(kin_syms)
# 2) entrez match
bt_ent = bt['Target Gene ID'].isin(kin_entrez)
matched = bt_sym | bt_ent

# Which kinome genes are found?
found_by_sym = set(bt.loc[bt_sym,'Target Gene Symbol']) & kin_syms
found_entrez = set(bt.loc[bt_ent,'Target Gene ID'])
kinome['found'] = False
kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol'].isin(found_by_sym),'found'] = True
kinome.loc[kinome['Entrez'].astype(str).isin(found_entrez),'found'] = True

missing = kinome[~kinome['found']]

# Build alias -> kinome symbol map from synonyms
alias_map = {}
for _,r in missing.iterrows():
    syns = str(r['Synonyms']).replace('nan','').split()
    for s in syns:
        alias_map[s.upper()] = r['Symbol']
    alias_map[r['Manning_Name'].upper()] = r['Symbol']

bt_upper = bt['Target Gene Symbol'].str.upper()
alias_hits = bt_upper.isin(alias_map.keys())
found_aliases = bt.loc[alias_hits,'Target Gene Symbol'].unique()

# map them
for bsym in found_aliases:
    kinsym = alias_map[bsym.upper()]
    kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol']==kinsym,'found'] = True

# Build Brunello-symbol -> canonical kinome-symbol map
bru2kin = {}
for s in kin_syms:
    bru2kin[s] = s                      # direct
for bsym in found_aliases:
    bru2kin[bsym] = alias_map[bsym.upper()]
bru2kin['PRPF4B'] = 'PRP4K'

# mark resolved genes
kinome.loc[kinome['Symbol']=='PRP4K','found'] = True

# subset Brunello rows
sel = bt[bt['Target Gene Symbol'].isin(bru2kin.keys())].copy()
sel['Kinome_Symbol'] = sel['Target Gene Symbol'].map(bru2kin)
sel = sel.merge(kinome[['Symbol','Group','Family']], left_on='Kinome_Symbol', right_on='Symbol', how='left').drop(columns='Symbol')

# Remove false RP1->WHR1 mapping
sel = sel[~((sel['Kinome_Symbol']=='WHR1') & (sel['Target Gene Symbol']=='RP1'))].copy()
del bru2kin['RP1']

# Reorder/clean columns for the deliverable
sel = sel.rename(columns={'sgRNA Target Sequence':'sgRNA_sequence','Target Context Sequence':'context_30mer',
    'PAM Sequence':'PAM','Rule Set 2 score':'RuleSet2_score','Target Gene Symbol':'Brunello_symbol',
    'Target Transcript':'transcript','Genomic Sequence':'chromosome','Strand':'strand',
    'Position of Base After Cut (1-based)':'cut_pos','Exon Number':'exon'})
sel['sgRNA_id'] = sel['Kinome_Symbol']+'_'+sel.groupby('Kinome_Symbol').cumcount().add(1).astype(str)
cols = ['sgRNA_id','Kinome_Symbol','Group','Family','Brunello_symbol','sgRNA_sequence','PAM',
        'context_30mer','RuleSet2_score','chromosome','cut_pos','strand','exon','transcript']
kin_guides = sel[cols].sort_values(['Group','Kinome_Symbol','sgRNA_id']).reset_index(drop=True)
kin_guides.to_csv('kinase_guides_brunello.csv', index=False)
print("Saved kinase_guides_brunello.csv:", kin_guides.shape)

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

kinome = pd.read_csv("{{artifact:cd56c267-e072-4096-a342-d491772117bf}}")
kin_guides = pd.read_csv("{{artifact:ba3ad2b7-99f4-4aa5-a9e9-745c93eab2a7}}")

denovo = ['GRK1','STK38','PRKY','CDK8']
kinome['found'] = ~kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)
kinome['needs_denovo_design'] = kinome['Symbol'].isin(denovo)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(13,5))
# Left: coverage bar per group (found vs de-novo needed)
grp_tot = kinome.groupby('Group').size()
grp_found = kinome.groupby('Group')['found'].sum()
grp_miss = grp_tot - grp_found
order = grp_tot.sort_values(ascending=False).index
ax=axes[0]
ax.bar(order, grp_found[order], color='#2c7fb8', edgecolor='black', lw=0.5, label='In Brunello (4 guides)')
ax.bar(order, grp_miss[order], bottom=grp_found[order], color='#d95f0e', edgecolor='black', lw=0.5, label='Needs de-novo design')
ax.set_ylabel('Kinase genes'); ax.set_title('Brunello coverage of kinome by group')
ax.legend(fontsize=9); ax.set_xticklabels(order, rotation=30, ha='right')
for s in ['top','right']: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)

# Right: guides per gene histogram (all 4)
ax=axes[1]
gpg_final = kin_guides.groupby('Kinome_Symbol').size()
vc = gpg_final.value_counts().sort_index()
bars=ax.bar(vc.index.astype(str), vc.values, color='#2ca25f', edgecolor='black', width=0.6)
ax.bar_label(bars, padding=2)
ax.set_xlabel('sgRNAs per gene'); ax.set_ylabel('Number of genes')
ax.set_title(f'sgRNA coverage: {len(kin_guides)} guides across {gpg_final.size} genes')
for s in ['top','right']: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)
fig.tight_layout(); fig.savefig('guides_per_gene.png', dpi=150)
